_comment: This file is OVERWRITTEN each security review. Do NOT append to it.

ssh_attacks.fail2ban_status: active - sshd jail running, 6 currently banned, 4228 total banned
ssh_attacks.change_since_last: fail2ban total bans 4228 (up from 4128, +100). 6 IPs currently banned (down from 8). 16263 failed passwords (+349). Continued brute-force from botnets (2.57.121.x, 45.148.10.x, 80.94.92.x). Only authorized activity from novakj. Rejected ssh-rsa pubkey attempts from 139.19.117.130 — automated scanner.

pdf_security.innerHTML_note: 74 usages across 24 files — all safe: static templates, clearing operations, escaped user data, numeric-only interpolation, blob URLs, or hardcoded SVG. hyperlinks.js added 5 (1 static dialog, 1 clearing, 1 hardcoded entity, 1 static context menu, 1 link list panel with escapeHtml).

task_034_review.feature: hyperlink annotations — add, edit, detect, navigate links in PDFs
innerHTML_details: Line 360: dialogOverlay.innerHTML = static dialog template. SAFE.
Line 709: contextMenu.innerHTML = static context menu template. SAFE.

Line 835: panel.innerHTML = html — link list panel. User data escaped via escapeHtml(). SAFE for text context. title attribute uses escapeHtml() which doesn't escape double quotes — same known issue as merge.js/batch.js, CSP mitigated.

url_handling.user_entered_urls: Forced to https:// prefix (line 504), prevents javascript: URIs. SAFE.
url_handling.detected_pdf_urls: Passed to window.open() without protocol validation (line 684). Malicious PDFs could contain javascript: URIs. CSP script-src 'self' + noopener/noreferrer mitigate. LOW-MEDIUM risk.
url_handling.email_urls: Validated with regex, prefixed with mailto:. SAFE.

task_034_review.input_validation: URL format enforced, email regex validated, page number bounds-checked against totalPages.
task_034_review.memory_management: Maps cleared on pdf:ready. Undo stacks capped at 40 entries per page.

jszip_min_js.purpose: ZIP file creation for split, convert, batch, and extract-images module downloads

known_issues: server_tokens set to 'build' - leaks nginx version (nginx/1.26.3 Ubuntu) in Server header. Should be 'off'.
No general *.md nginx block - only CLAUDE.md specifically blocked. Any new .md file added to webroot would be publicly accessible.
UFW firewall is inactive - only SSH and HTTP/HTTPS ports are needed but no firewall enforces this.
disable_symlinks off in nginx config - low risk since only novakj can write to webroot.
CGI location block exists in nginx but no CGI scripts present - dead config that could be removed.
Codex proxy at /codex returns 502 - backend not running. No security risk but dead endpoint.

merge.js, batch.js, and hyperlinks.js use escapeHtml() in attribute contexts (title/aria-label) which does not escape double quotes. CSP script-src 'self' mitigates inline handler injection. Should add escapeAttr() function.

hyperlinks.js: detected PDF link URLs not validated for protocol scheme. A malicious PDF could contain javascript: URIs in link annotations. CSP + noopener/noreferrer mitigate. Should add protocol allowlist (http/https/mailto) before window.open().
img2pdf.js image validation uses OR logic (MIME type OR extension) - slightly permissive but acceptable for client-side processing.
insert-pages.js has dead code: escapeHtml() function defined but never called.
metadata.js has dead code: escapeHtml() function defined but never called.
batch.js line 294: escapeHtml() used with textContent causes double-escaping — cosmetic display bug for filenames containing &, <, >.
notes: Full security review completed — 58 checks passed, 0 critical findings.

NEW: Reviewed hyperlinks.js TASK-034 (hyperlink annotations): 5 innerHTML usages — 1 static dialog template, 1 clearing, 1 hardcoded entity, 1 static context menu, 1 link list panel with escapeHtml(). User-entered URLs forced to https:// prefix. Email addresses regex-validated. Page numbers bounds-checked. window.open uses noopener,noreferrer for URL links. Undo capped at 40. No external requests, no server storage. SECURE with one new known issue: detected PDF link URLs not protocol-validated.
innerHTML count: 74 across 24 files (hyperlinks.js adds 5 new usages).
All JS files scanned: no eval(), Function(), document.write() found. No sensitive files or symlinks in webroot.
SSH: fail2ban total bans 4228 (+100 since last review). 6 IPs currently banned (down from 8). 16263 failed passwords (+349). Continued brute-force from botnets. Only authorized activity from novakj.
SSL certificate valid until June 18, 2026 (77 days remaining).
Nginx config syntax valid. All security headers confirmed in HTTP response.
recommendations: MEDIUM-LOW: Add protocol allowlist to hyperlinks.js handleLinkClick() — only allow http:, https:, mailto: schemes before calling window.open() on detected PDF links
LOW: Add escapeAttr() function to merge.js, batch.js, and hyperlinks.js for attribute contexts instead of escapeHtml()
LOW: Fix batch.js line 294 — use fileName directly with textContent instead of escapeHtml(fileName)
LOW: Set server_tokens off in nginx.conf to stop leaking version info (currently exposes nginx/1.26.3)
LOW: Enable UFW firewall (sudo ufw allow 22,80,443/tcp && sudo ufw enable)
LOW: Add general *.md deny rule to nginx with explicit exception for known safe files
LOW: Remove dead escapeHtml() function from insert-pages.js and metadata.js
LOW: Remove unused CGI location block from nginx config
LOW: Consider disable_symlinks on in nginx for defense in depth
